title: 10 best herbal creams for sunburn

remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Aloe vera gel contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Hydrating and Moisturizing Properties: Aloe vera's high water content (up to 95%) helps to lock in moisture, soothing dry, itchy skin and reducing the appearance of peeling and flakiness caused by sunburn.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants present in aloe vera, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of long-term skin damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with sunburn because of its soothing and cooling properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloe-emodin and aloin, which reduce redness and swelling. Additionally, aloe vera has hydrating properties that help replenish lost moisture in sunburned skin, promoting faster healing. Its natural antioxidants also protect the skin from further damage, making it an effective remedy for alleviating sunburn discomfort and promoting skin recovery.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help with sunburn by reducing inflammation, soothing the skin, and promoting wound healing due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Glucomannans: Assist with sunburn by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing water loss, and providing relief from pain and itching due to their moisturizing and emollient properties.

Phenolic compounds (including C-glycosyl flavones): Help with sunburn by neutralizing free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting skin recovery due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part for making creams due to their high content of soothing and healing compounds such as aloin and aloe-emodin.
Gel from Leaves: The gel inside Aloe vera leaves is rich in vitamins and minerals, making it a popular ingredient in creams for its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: Aloe vera extract from leaves is used to make creams due to its high concentration of antioxidants and its ability to promote skin repair and regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200ml aloe vera gel, 50g shea butter, 25g coconut oil, 5g beeswax, and 2 tablespoons vitamin e oil.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler at 100-110 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Combine the aloe vera gel, shea butter, and vitamin e oil in a separate bowl, mixing until well combined.
Add the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture to the aloe vera mixture, stirring until smooth and creamy consistency is achieved.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool and set for 30 minutes to an hour before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream contains flavonoids, such as luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream, including kaempferol and quercetin, help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals and promote healing after a sunburn.

Soothing: Pot marigold has been traditionally used to soothe skin irritations and wounds, and its cream can provide relief from the discomfort and itching associated with sunburn, while also promoting skin hydration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The cream also promotes wound healing and tissue repair, helping to regenerate skin cells and restore its natural barrier. This makes Calendula creams a popular natural remedy for soothing and relieving sunburn, providing relief from itching, peeling, and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate redness associated with sunburn, promoting faster recovery and soothing the skin.

Carotenoids (especially Calendula-derived Carotenoids): These pigments, particularly Calendula-derived carotenoids, have antioxidant and protective properties that shield the skin from further damage, reducing the risk of blistering and promoting healing.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids found in Calendula officinalis exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help prevent infection and speed up the recovery process from sunburn, reducing discomfort and promoting the healing of damaged skin tissues.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to soothe and protect sunburned skin.
Leaves: Used for their calming and cooling effects to reduce redness and inflammation of sunburned skin.
Roots: Used for their soothing and protective properties to help regenerate skin cells and reduce sunburn damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula flowers on a sunny day when they are fully open to ensure potency.
Dry the calendula flowers in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the calendula powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler to infuse the oil.
Whip 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of the infused coconut oil using a hand mixer to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile cream helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with sunburn, soothing the skin and promoting healing.
Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals generated by UV radiation, preventing premature aging and skin cell death.

Soothing and calming: Chamomile's apigenin content has a direct effect on the skin's receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing stress-induced redness and irritation, making it an excellent topical treatment for sunburn relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with sunburn because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, calm and cool the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with sunburn. Its natural moisturizing properties also help to hydrate and protect the skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of further irritation. This makes Matricaria chamomilla creams a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with sunburn by reducing inflammation and pain through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and discomfort.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it beneficial for sunburn relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe sunburned skin.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in the healing of sunburned skin.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their rich content of essential oils, which possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate sunburn discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers at full bloom for maximum potency and efficiency of extraction process.
Dry the harvested flowers using a low-temperature dehydration method for 24 hours to preserve their properties.
Mix 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 100 grams of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Infuse the mixture for 2 hours under gentle heat to facilitate the extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture and whip it with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of emulsifying wax for a smooth texture.

remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing burned skin.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV rays, reducing the risk of prolonged healing time and potential scarring.
Calming and anti-irritating properties: The calming effects of lavender oil can help ease itching and discomfort associated with sunburn, promoting a sense of relaxation and relief for affected skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The key compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn. The cooling and calming effects of lavender creams can provide instant relief from the discomfort and heat of sunburned skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of further damage. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to soothe sunburned skin.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn, promoting faster recovery.
Linalyl acetate: An ester of linalool, which has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in soothing and calming sunburned skin, reducing pain and discomfort.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that helps protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, promoting skin repair and reducing sunburn severity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for sunburn due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are used due to their soothing and antiseptic properties that help to reduce redness and promote healing.
Seeds: They are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in the recovery and prevention of further irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency and fragrance.
Infuse the dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 1 cup of beeswax and 1 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow extracts contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Soil-thickening and moisturizing: The mucilage in marshmallows creates a protective barrier on the skin that soothes and locks in moisture, providing relief from dryness and irritation caused by sun exposure.
Antioxidant: Marshmallow extracts contain antioxidants like vitamin E, which help neutralize free radicals formed during sunburn, reducing oxidative stress and promoting wound healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with sunburn because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilage in the plant creates a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing heat and discomfort. It also helps to calm redness and inflammation, promoting skin healing and reducing the risk of blistering. Additionally, the cooling effect of the cream provides instant relief from the burning sensation, making it an effective natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help with sunburn by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Gallic acid: Gallic acid has antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and promoting skin health.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm sunburned skin.
Leaves: They are used for their moisturizing and cooling effects, which can help to reduce redness and discomfort associated with sunburn.

Seeds (not actually a main ingredient in most sunburn creams, but a few creams might contain it): Althaea officinalis seeds are not typically used as the main ingredient in sunburn creams, but some herbal remedies may use them for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, however, it's worth noting that seeds aren't as commonly used as the roots and leaves in sunburn creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried althaea officinalis root from a reliable supplier or cultivate your own.
Combine the dried root with 500ml of cold water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes to extract the root's medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl discarding the solids.
Mix the cooled liquid with 200g of shea butter and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain contains anti-inflammatory agents like allantoin, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn by reducing inflammation in the skin.

Antioxidants: Plantain's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals generated during sun exposure, reducing the risk of long-term skin damage and promoting faster healing.
Skin-soothing glycosides: Plantain contains glycosides that have a cooling effect on the skin, helping to calm and soothe sunburned skin, reduce itching and discomfort, and promote skin hydration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plantain plant contains allantoin, a compound that promotes skin regeneration and reduces redness. Allantoin also helps to speed up the healing process of sunburned skin by reducing inflammation and promoting the repair of damaged skin cells. Additionally, plantain creams can help to calm and cool the skin, providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with sunburn. This makes plantain creams a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Aucubin: Aucubin, a phenylethanoid glycoside, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Asteroside: Asteroside, a triterpene saponin, has antioxidant properties that may help protect skin cells from oxidative stress caused by UV radiation, contributing to the healing of sunburned skin.
Rhamnose: Rhamnose, a sugar molecule found in Plantago major, has antioxidant properties that may help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and protective properties for sunburned skin.
Seeds: The seeds contain a high amount of plant sterols, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin regeneration.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to calm and repair sun-damaged skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh or dried plantago major leaves in the morning when dew is still present.
Combine the harvested leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Mix the resulting infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil then heat.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Red clover contains anti-inflammatory compounds like isoflavones, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn by minimizing inflammation.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in red clover cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting faster healing.

Vasoactive compounds: Red clover's vasoactive compounds, including isoflavones and saponins, help improve blood flow to the affected area, which promotes healing, reduces redness, and soothes discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover, creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in red clover, such as isoflavones and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn. These compounds help to soothe and calm the skin, promoting healing and reducing discomfort. Additionally, red clover creams may also have a cooling effect, providing relief from the heat and pain associated with sunburn. This makes it a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Isorhapontigenin: This flavonoid helps with sunburn by exhibiting antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation in the skin.
Quercetin: A polyphenolic flavonoid, quercetin helps to alleviate sunburn symptoms by reducing inflammation, limiting oxidative stress, and promoting skin repair.
Kaempferol: This flavonoid has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to mitigate sunburn damage by reducing skin redness and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, particularly isoflavones, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe sunburned skin.
Flowers: Containing triterpenoids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce redness and promote skin healing.
Seeds: Rich in isoflavones and other phytochemicals, which can help protect the skin from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried trifolium pratense flowers and leaves on a sunny day in the summer months.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 10 minutes to make a strong tea.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the trifolium pratense tea with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10 minutes or until it reaches a consistent temperature of 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and set for 30 minutes before using.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with sunburn by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, providing relief from discomfort.
Hypericin: Has antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and skin damage.
Flavonoids: Act as a natural anti-histamine, blocking the release of histamine and reducing allergic reactions, itching, and inflammation associated with sunburn.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. By soothing and protecting the skin, Hypericum perforatum creams can help to accelerate the healing process, reduce blistering, and promote the return of the skin's natural barrier function, providing relief and comfort to sunburned skin.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and damage caused by UV radiation, thus alleviating sunburn symptoms.

Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to neutralize free radicals, reduce inflammation, and promote skin repair, ultimately soothing sunburned skin.

N-feruloylserotonin: This phenolic compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from further damage, reduce redness and swelling, and promote skin healing in sunburned areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, making them suitable for sunburn creams.
Stems: The stems of Hypericum perforatum contain a combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh flowers of hypericum perforatum on a dry morning after the dew has evaporated completely.
Dry the fresh flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for at least 2 weeks.
Mix 20g of dried flowers with 100g of base cream containing emulsifiers and stabilizers in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler to 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes while constantly stirring the mixture.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and allow it to cool down completely.

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Help to prevent infection and reduce inflammation by killing bacteria, viruses, and fungi on the skin's surface.
Aloe Vera's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Soothe and calm sunburned skin by reducing redness, swelling, and pain caused by inflammation.
Green Tea's Antioxidant Properties: Neutralize free radicals that can cause further damage to the skin after sun exposure, promoting faster healing and reducing the appearance of sunburn.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredient, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn. It also helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, tea tree creams have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote skin healing. This makes tea tree creams a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This monoterpenic ketone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Limonene: A cyclic terpene with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, limonene aids in reducing the risk of infection and promotes healing in sunburned skin.
Cymene: A monoterpenic hydrocarbon with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, cymene helps to soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high concentrations of the active compound melaleuca oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Buds: They are rich in melaleuca oil, which helps to soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Stems: They contain the same beneficial compounds as the leaves and buds, making them a useful source of melaleuca oil for sunburn creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100c for 2 hours using a 1:5 ratio.
Mix 10g of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil with 90g of a base oil such as sweet almond oil.
Blend the mixture thoroughly for 5 minutes using a hand mixer or whisk.
Filter the final product through a 100 micron filter into clean glass jars for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, which helps prevent further damage to the skin and reduces the risk of long-term skin problems like premature aging and skin cancer.

Analgesic properties: The analgesic compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and sesquiterpenes, help relieve itching, stinging, and discomfort associated with sunburn, providing fast and effective pain relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn. These compounds also have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from further damage and promote healing. Additionally, ginger creams can help to soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from the discomfort and pain of sunburn. This makes ginger creams a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help soothe sunburned skin and promote its natural healing process.

Curcuminoids: These polyphenolic compounds, particularly curcumin, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help neutralize free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and alleviate symptoms of sunburn.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale in creams for sunburn is its Rhyzomes, as they contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Roots: Zingiber officinale roots are also used, particularly for their ability to reduce redness and inflammation associated with sunburn.
Leaves: The leaves of Zingiber officinale may also be used, as they contain antioxidants that can help protect and soothe sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Grate the washed zingiber officinale using a microplane grater for 5 minutes to release oils.
Combine 10g of beeswax with 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted for 10 minutes.
Steep the grated zingiber officinale in the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The licorice extract contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which calms redness and swelling caused by sun exposure. Additionally, licorice has been shown to have antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate sun damage. When applied topically, herbal licorice creams provide instant relief from sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and promoting a faster recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice extract has been shown to reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing redness and swelling, and helping to relieve discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Licorice contains antioxidants like glycyrrhizin, which help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals generated during UV radiation, promoting healthy skin recovery.

Soothing and calming effects: The saponins present in licorice extract have a calming effect on the skin, reducing itching, irritation, and discomfort associated with sunburn, while also helping to restore the skin's natural barrier function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin.
Barks: Contain glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and irritation caused by sunburn, promoting faster healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder from a reputable supplier for future use.
Combine 100 milliliters of distilled water and 10 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it is fully incorporated at a temperature of 70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Mix in 20 milliliters of glycerin and 2 grams of vitamin e oil with a spatula for an additional 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool before refrigerating for at least 24 hours for proper solidification.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort creams helps with sunburn because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as centella asiatica, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort creams contain moisturizing agents that hydrate the skin, reducing peeling and discomfort. This natural formula provides rapid relief from sunburn pain, itchiness, and dryness, allowing the skin to recover quickly and return to its normal state.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Asiatic pennywort contains compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant: The cream's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting faster recovery.

Wound healing: Asiatic pennywort has been shown to accelerate wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and reducing scar formation, making it an effective treatment for sunburn-related skin damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain potent natural antibacterial agents that prevent infection and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest centella asiatica leaves and flowers at dawn, ensuring 100g of fresh material for subsequent processing.
Blend the harvested centella asiatica material with distilled water in a ratio of 1:10 and leave it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and combine the extracted liquid with 20% glycerin, 15% beeswax, and 5% vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes under stirring until the ingredients are fully incorporated and a smooth consistency is achieved.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken before filling it into sterilized jars, with a shelf life of six months when stored in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with sunburn because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the skin. The natural antioxidants present in sage work to reduce redness and swelling caused by UV radiation, promoting a quicker recovery from sun damage. Additionally, sage's emollient qualities moisturize and hydrate the skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort associated with sunburned areas. As a result, using herbal sage creams can provide fast relief and promote healthy-looking skin even after exposure to the sun.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, preventing further damage to the skin and promoting healing.
Wound-healing agents: Sage cream contains compounds that stimulate collagen production and improve wound healing, which can aid in the recovery process from sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain flavonoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in sun-damaged skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves and dry them for at least 24 hours in a well-ventilated area.
Combine 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove any solids and filter it again if necessary.
Pour the final product into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The active compound curcumin in turmeric cream reduces inflammation and redness caused by UV exposure, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of peeling. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect damaged skin cells from further harm, leaving sunburned skin looking healthier and more radiant with continued use.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation caused by sunburn, leading to faster healing and reduced pain.
Antioxidant: Curcumin and other antioxidants in turmeric creams neutralize free radicals produced by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting natural recovery.
Wound-healing: Turmeric's antimicrobial and antiseptic properties help prevent infection, promote collagen synthesis, and accelerate wound healing, reducing the appearance of sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it an effective ingredient in soothing and protecting against sunburn.
Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are rich in volatile oils that have natural analgesic and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce pain, redness, and inflammation caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including curcumin powder, coconut oil, beeswax, and aloe vera gel in equal proportions.
Heat coconut oil in a double boiler or a heatproof bowl set over simmering water for 10-15 minutes.
Add beeswax to the melted coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated, taking approximately 5 minutes.
Stir in curcumin powder and aloe vera gel mixture into the heated oil-wax mixture, allowing it to infuse for 20-30 minutes.
Remove from heat, let it cool slightly, then whip with an electric mixer or whisk until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with sunburn because they contain natural ingredients that soothe and calm the skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry, a key ingredient in many herbal elder creams, reduce redness and swelling caused by sun exposure. Additionally, the moisturizing effects of the cream help to hydrate parched skin, reducing peeling and flakiness associated with sunburn. As a result, herbal elder creams provide rapid relief from discomfort and promote healthy skin regeneration, allowing users to recover quickly from a sunburn and enjoy the great outdoors again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder cream contains flavonoids, anthocyanins, and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease redness associated with sunburn, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in elder cream, such as vitamin C, beta-carotene, and polyphenols, help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, which can damage skin cells and accelerate aging. By neutralizing these free radicals, elder cream helps protect the skin from further damage.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in elder cream, such as triterpenes and flavonoids, help combat bacterial infections that can occur when the skin is damaged by sunburn, reducing the risk of infection and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes contain flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce inflammation and promote the healing process of sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers at dawn and wash them thoroughly with cold water to remove any impurities.
Dry the washed flowers in a warm oven set to 150°f for 12 hours or until crispy to preserve their potency.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried sambucus nigra flowers with 1 cup of distilled water in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 8 hours.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into another container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 1/2 cup of heavy cream and store the resulting sambucus nigra cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers creams helps with sunburn because they contain natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb cleavers has been used for centuries to treat skin conditions, and its inclusion in creams provides rapid relief from redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by sun exposure. The cream's cooling effect also helps to reduce heat and itching, allowing the skin to heal faster and minimizing the risk of peeling or blistering.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cleavers has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm irritated skin, potentially reducing redness and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant: The herb is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by UV radiation, which may contribute to accelerated aging and sun-related issues.

Wound-healing properties: Cleavers has been traditionally used to treat wounds and skin ulcers due to its ability to promote tissue repair and regeneration. This property may also be beneficial for accelerating the healing process in cases of mild sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which soothe and calm skin irritations caused by sunburn.
Rhizomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems for approximately one hour in early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for six hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried galium aparine with 50 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for approximately one hour until melted and smooth.
Pour the creamy mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow to cool at room temperature for four hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with sunburn because they contain allantoin, a natural ingredient that accelerates skin regeneration and promotes healing. Allantoin works by stimulating collagen production, reducing inflammation, and soothing itchy skin. Additionally, comfrey's rich content of vitamins A, C, and E provides antioxidant protection, helping to repair damaged skin cells and prevent further damage from UV radiation. As a result, comfrey creams can provide quick relief for sunburned skin, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort while promoting a faster recovery time.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey's allantoin content helps to accelerate the skin's natural regeneration process, promoting faster healing and reducing the appearance of sunburned skin.

Vitamins A and E: The high levels of vitamins A and E in comfrey cream provide antioxidant protection, helping to neutralize free radicals caused by UV damage and reducing inflammation and redness associated with sunburn.
Alantolactone: Comfrey's alantolactone compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with severe sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain allantoin, a natural compound that helps accelerate skin regeneration and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried symphytum officinale herb, witch hazel, and sweet almond oil for infusion.
Combine one part dried symphytum officinale herb with two parts boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to extract the medicinal properties of the symphytum officinale herb.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids and measuring out one part infused liquid with three parts sweet almond oil.

Mix the infused liquid and sweet almond oil blend with 10% witch hazel solution in a double boiler, stirring constantly until the mixture reaches a comfortable consistency for application within 30 minutes of preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with sunburn because of its unique composition, which combines the anti-inflammatory properties of burdock root extract with the moisturizing benefits of natural oils. The extract soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with sunburn. Meanwhile, the cream's emollients lock in moisture to hydrate parched skin, promoting faster healing and minimizing peeling. As a result, burdock creams provide effective relief from sunburn discomfort, helping to restore healthy-looking skin in no time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Burdock cream's anti-inflammatory compound, lignan, helps reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn by inhibiting prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of burdock cream help neutralize free radicals generated by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting healing.

Soothing and protective property: Burdock's mucilages and polysaccharides create a soothing film on the skin that helps to calm and protect it from irritation, reducing discomfort and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and antioxidants that moisturize and protect the skin from further damage caused by sun exposure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arctium lappa roots and dry them for at least 14 days to ensure potency and stability.
Grind 30g of dried arctium lappa root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or blender.
Mix the powdered arctium lappa with 100g of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 65°c to 70°c.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then pour into sterilized glass containers and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with sunburn because they possess potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin. The cream's natural antioxidants and flavonoids work to neutralize free radicals, reducing redness and swelling associated with sunburned skin. Additionally, dandelion's aloe-like properties provide long-lasting hydration, helping to replenish moisture lost due to sun exposure. This combination of benefits allows for rapid relief from sunburn discomfort, promoting faster healing and preventing further damage. As a result, herbal dandelion creams offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating the symptoms of sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage.

Skin soothing and moisturizing properties: Dandelion cream's fatty acid content, including linoleic acid and oleic acid, can help soothe and moisturize sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and soothing sun-damaged skin.
Seeds: Containing high levels of fatty acids and omega-3 oils, they help moisturize and repair damaged skin, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves according to local regulations.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until crispy.
Combine 200g dried dandelion flowers and leaves with 400ml sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture for 4 hours stirring occasionally or until the oil reaches 55°c to 60°c.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and store in the refrigerator.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for sunburn are topical preparations made from plant extracts that help alleviate the symptoms of sunburn, such as redness, itching, and inflammation. These creams are formulated with various herbs, including Calendula officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe barbadensis, Symphytum officinale, Arnica montana, Hamamelis virginiana, Aloe vera, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Melaleuca alternifolia, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, sunburn can lead to discomfort, increased risk of skin cancer, and long-term skin damage, affecting daily life, causing pain, and limiting social activities.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sunburn that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations such as eczema, minor burns, and abrasions. Herbal creams can provide soothing relief for these conditions by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and calming the skin. Ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula are commonly used in herbal creams to treat these ailments, offering natural and gentle alternatives to traditional treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with sunburn is a soothing blend of Aloe Vera, Calendula, and Chamomile. Aloe Vera's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, while Calendula accelerates the healing process and promotes skin regeneration. Chamomile's anti-irritant properties calm the skin and provide relief from itching and discomfort. By applying these creams together, you can effectively alleviate sunburn symptoms, promote skin healing, and reduce the risk of long-term damage. Regular application can help to restore your skin's natural health and radiance.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and swelling. Calendula also promotes wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis and improving skin barrier function. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help prevent infection and promote a healthy recovery. Overall, Calendula officinalis creams can provide quick relief and support the natural healing process of sunburned skin.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: They help protect the skin from further damage by absorbing UV radiation and reducing inflammation.
Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and pain.

Naphthoquinones: They possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in sunburned skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of further damage.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and protect sunburned skin.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in healing sunburn.
Seeds: They contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce redness and swelling in sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers and 200 milliliters of jojoba oil.
Steep the dried calendula flowers in the jojoba oil for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove the flowers.
Add 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter to the infused oil mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes or until the ingredients are fully incorporated.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with sunburn because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, while also promoting the healing of damaged skin. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the skin also makes it easier to rehydrate and replenish the skin's natural moisture barrier, accelerating the recovery process and reducing the risk of further irritation or infection.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with sunburn due to its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Quercetin: Exhibits antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and promote skin repair in sunburned skin.
Kaempferol: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that aid in soothing sunburned skin, reducing redness, and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are rich in flavonoids and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe sunburned skin.
Flowers: Flowers contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in wound healing and reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.

Stems: Stems of Hypericum perforatum contain bioflavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation, promote collagen production, and improve skin elasticity, making them beneficial for sunburn relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried hypericum perforatum flowers at the peak of blooming to ensure optimal potency.
Grind 2 parts of dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of beeswax with 2 parts of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Combine 1 part of powdered dried flowers with 1 part of vitamin e oil in a small bowl.
Blend the powdered flower mixture with the beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with sunburn because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling. The gel extracted from Aloe vera has soothing properties that calm the skin, providing instant relief from sunburn pain. It also has cooling and hydrating effects that help to rehydrate the skin, reducing the risk of peeling and promoting faster healing. This natural remedy is rich in vitamins and minerals that promote skin health, making it an effective solution for alleviating sunburn symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps with sunburn by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged skin.
Aloe-emodin: Assists in reducing inflammation, pain, and redness associated with sunburn, while also promoting skin regeneration.
Anthraquinones: Provides antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, and also aid in soothing and calming sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high amounts of gel that can be extracted and used in creams to provide cooling and soothing relief for sunburned skin.
Gel from Leaves: Aloe vera gel is extracted from the leaves, and it's known for its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help to calm and heal sunburned skin.

Extract from Leaves: The gel-like extract from the leaves of Aloe barbadensis contains vitamins, minerals, and other compounds that help to reduce redness, inflammation, and pain associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and 10 grams of glycerin in a mixing bowl.
Mix the ingredients for 5 minutes using a hand mixer until well combined and smooth.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of candelilla wax to the bowl and heat in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Remove the bowl from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes then whip the mixture using a hand mixer until light and fluffy.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it set for 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn. The cream's soothing and moisturizing effects calm the skin, promoting a faster recovery. The active compound, allantoin, also helps to repair damaged skin cells, promoting a more even skin tone. Additionally, Symphytum officinale creams can aid in the reduction of pain and discomfort caused by sunburn, allowing the skin to heal more efficiently. This natural remedy provides relief and promotes skin recovery.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps with sunburn by promoting skin regeneration and reducing inflammation.
Mucilages: Helps with sunburn by providing soothing and protective effects on damaged skin, reducing redness and pain.
Polysaccharides: Helps with sunburn by stimulating the production of collagen, promoting skin healing and reducing the risk of blistering.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Symphytum officinale contain allantoin, a compound that promotes skin healing and regeneration.
Stems: The stems of Symphytum officinale are rich in mucilage, a soothing agent that helps to reduce inflammation and protect the skin.
Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale contain allantoin and other compounds that aid in skin regeneration and protection against sun damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves from a clean and pesticide-free area by cutting them at base with scissors.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris by soaking for 30 minutes in cold water.
Infuse the cleaned leaves in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2 for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and a natural emulsifier at a ratio of 20% beeswax and 5% emulsifier to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plant's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, help to reduce redness and swelling, promoting a faster recovery from sunburn. Arnica creams also have a cooling effect, which can provide instant relief from the discomfort and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, it helps to repair damaged skin cells, reducing the risk of peeling and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Helenalin: This sesquiterpene lactone helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with sunburn by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Nordihydroguaiaretic acid (NDGA): As a phenolic compound, NDGA has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Isoalantolactone: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and bacterial infections associated with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Arnica montana for making creams due to their high concentration of arnica oil, which contains anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, although to a lesser extent, as they contain some of the same active compounds as the flowers, providing additional anti-inflammatory and soothing benefits.

Stems: Some creams may also use the stems of Arnica montana, as they contain a lower concentration of active compounds compared to the flowers and leaves but still offer some anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried arnica montana flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Heat the mixture over low heat stirring occasionally until the beeswax and shea butter are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's extract, witch hazel, has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated skin. It contains tannins and gallic acid, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn. The cream's cooling and astringent effects also help to calm the skin, promoting the healing process and reducing discomfort caused by sunburn. This natural remedy can provide relief from sunburned skin, making it a popular choice for after-sun care.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress caused by UV radiation, which contributes to sunburn symptoms.
Tannins: Tannins have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and discomfort.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the skin from further damage and promote healing of sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of astringent tannins, which help to soothe and protect the skin from sun damage.
Barks: The bark contains a compound called hamamelitannin, which has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that aid in reducing sunburn and promoting skin healing.
Roots: The roots are rich in hamamelitannin, which helps to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote the healing of sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in the morning to preserve their potency.
Infuse 250ml of carrier oil with 100g of the dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Combine 50g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and 50ml of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer until it reaches a smooth creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with sunburn because they are rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene and other antioxidants that soothe and calm the skin. The gel-like substance in aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, aloe vera's hydrating properties help to replenish moisture lost due to sunburn, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of peeling and scarring. This natural remedy provides quick relief and promotes skin recovery.
remedy_constituents: Glycoproteins: These help with sunburn by promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and soothing the skin.
Anthraquinones (specifically Aloin and Aloe-emodin): These constituents help with sunburn by reducing inflammation, preventing oxidative stress, and promoting the repair of damaged skin cells.
Vitamins (such as Vitamin E and Vitamin C): These help with sunburn by neutralizing free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting the healing of damaged skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part due to their high concentration of gel, which provides soothing and moisturizing properties.
Stems: The inner part of the stem is sometimes used, as it contains a higher concentration of aloe's medicinal properties compared to the outer leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 aloe vera leaves from mature plants for their gel and extract aloe vera's medicinal properties.
Grind 1 aloe vera leaf into a pulp in a blender and squeeze out 1 cup of gel from it.
Mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Combine 1 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of aloe vera gel and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a separate bowl.
Blend the oil mixture with the gel mixture and pour into jars for cooling and solidifying within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with sunburn because they contain licorice extract, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The saponins present in the cream help to cool and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with sunburn. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the skin, promoting the healing process and reducing the risk of further irritation. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra creams an effective natural remedy for relieving sunburn symptoms and promoting skin recovery.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Flavonoids (specifically, isoflavonoids and flavanones): They exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the skin from further damage and promote healing.

Licorice acid (glycyrrhizic acid): It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, while also promoting skin repair and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high glycyrrhizic acid content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe sunburned skin.
Stems: Some creams might use stems for their additional glycyrrhizic acid content, which provides similar benefits to the roots.
Leaves: Leaves may be used in some creams due to their potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, although they are less commonly used than roots and stems.
remedy_recipe: Measure 500g of distilled water and 100g of glycerin to create a base solution.
Combine 10g of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Gradually add 200g of distilled water to the double boiler containing the glycyrrhiza glabra mixture.
Whisk 100g of shea butter and 50g of coconut oil into the mixture to enhance moisturizing properties.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to glass jars for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with sunburn because they contain high concentrations of tea tree oil, a natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. This oil has been shown to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with sunburn. Additionally, tea tree oil has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection and promote healing of the affected area, allowing the skin to recover from the burn more quickly and effectively. This makes Melaleuca alternifolia creams a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with sunburn by reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Linalool: Reduces inflammation and prevents further damage to the skin, promoting healing and regeneration of damaged tissue.
Methyl eugenol: Exhibits antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals and protecting the skin from oxidative stress caused by sun exposure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The essential oil extracted from Melaleuca alternifolia leaves is primarily used in creams for sunburn relief due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Seeds: While not as commonly used as the leaves, some products may incorporate Melaleuca alternifolia seed extracts to aid in wound healing and skin regeneration.
Buds: Tea tree oil, which can be derived from Melaleuca alternifolia buds, is sometimes added to sunburn creams to help soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine 50 grams of distilled water and 10 grams of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in a separate bowl.
Mix the distilled water and essential oil mixture into the beeswax and coconut oil.
Heat the mixture at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes with constant stirring.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. It also promotes skin regeneration and accelerates the healing process by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have a protective effect on the skin, reducing damage caused by UV radiation and promoting a healthy skin environment. This makes Ginkgo biloba creams a popular natural remedy for soothing and healing sunburn.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce inflammation and promote healing by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Terpenoids (specifically, bilobalide and ginkgolides): Have antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting skin cell regeneration.

Phenolic acids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help protect the skin from damage caused by UV radiation and reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe sunburned skin.
Seeds: The seeds contain bilobalide and ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that aid in reducing sunburn pain and inflammation.
Barks: The barks have a rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves dried and 120 grams of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Infuse 30 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves in 120 grams of carrier oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of carrier oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Whip the cooled mixture from step 3 with 10 grams of vitamin e oil until it thickens into a smooth cream.
